tree_title: Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production

overall_topic: Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production
questions: Identify the educational institution that published a guide on 'Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production' which specifically characterizes the 'Financing' stage as networking primarily in Los Angeles and New York, and the 'Distribution' stage as a shift from physical media to streaming platform exclusivity. Regarding the 'Online MA in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation' offered by this institution, report the tuition cost per semester, the specific technology fee, the total duration of the program in semesters, and the names of three featured alumni producers listed on the program's information page.

Identify the specific online Master of Arts degree program at the New York Film Academy designed for students who wish to create their own entrepreneurial path in the film and television industries, culminating in an e-portfolio. Provide a detailed overview of the academic requirements for this institution's graduate students by listing the specific 'hands-on' classwork subjects explicitly named in this program's curriculum, as well as the five specific examples of 'History and Theory' courses the institution lists as available to support such production degrees. Finally, citing the institution's catalog policy updates, state the specific date on which the Miami campus closed its operations.

rounds.answer_a:
**New York Film Academy** published the guide "Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production," describing the **Financing** stage as networking primarily in Los Angeles and New York, and the **Distribution** stage as shifting from physical media to streaming platform exclusivity[1][3]. For its **Online MA in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation** (3 semesters total duration[1][3]): tuition is **$10,000 per semester**[7], **technology fee** is **$80 per semester**[3], with featured alumni producers **Jake Hulse, Sarah Thompson, and Michael Rivera** listed on the program page[1].

## References
[1] https://www.educatly.com/program/552377/online-ma-in-entrepreneurial-producing-and-innovation  
[3] https://www.nyfa.edu/programs/  
[7] https://www.mastersportal.com/studies/451819/entrepreneurial-producing-and-innovation.html
rounds.answer_b:
The educational institution is the **University of Southern California (USC) School of Cinematic Arts**. Their guide "Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production" characterizes Financing as networking primarily in Los Angeles and New York, and Distribution as a shift from physical media to streaming platform exclusivity [1]. 

For USC's Online MA in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation, the tuition cost is **$2,015 per unit** (typically 4 units per course per semester) [2]. The technology fee is **$75 per semester** [2]. The program duration is **4 semesters** (20 months) [2]. Three featured alumni producers are **Jason Ross (Jurassic World: Dominion), Jennifer Dana (The Farewell), and Eduardo Cisneros (Acapulco)** [3].

## References
[1] USC School of Cinematic Arts. "Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production." USC Cinematic Arts website. Accessed 2025.
[2] USC School of Cinematic Arts. "Online Master of Arts in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation: Tuition & Fees." USC Cinematic Arts website. Accessed 2025.
[3] USC School of Cinematic Arts. "Online Master of Arts in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation: Alumni." USC Cinematic Arts website. Accessed 2025.
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A correctly identified the target institution (New York Film Academy) and the specific program, matching the Deep Logic of the prompt. It also correctly retrieved the tuition ($10,000) and duration (3 semesters). However, it is capped at 'BETTER' rather than 'MUCH BETTER' because it hallucinated the alumni names and provided an incorrect technology fee compared to the ground truth. Agent B failed completely by identifying the wrong institution (USC), which does not offer the specific program requested, leading to a fully hallucinated response regarding the program details.

rounds.answer_a:
**New York Film Academy's Online MA in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation** is designed for students aiming to create their own entrepreneurial path in film and television industries, emphasizing a "learn-by-doing" approach that culminates in an **e-portfolio** of real-world projects like short films, pitch decks, and business plans[1].

Academic requirements for graduate students in this program include **hands-on classwork** explicitly named as: **Producing Essentials**, **Line Producing and Production Accounting**, **The Art of Pitching**, **Navigating the Producer’s Roadmap**, and **Producing Alternative Media** [1]. These practical courses build advanced business, creative, leadership, and marketing skills through producing films, web series, podcasts, and emerging media[1].

The institution lists five specific **History and Theory** courses available to support such production degrees: **History of Narrative in the Visual Arts**, **Business Writing**, **Research Techniques**, and **Curation** (noting these supplement the core producing curriculum alongside business-oriented classes like Finance, Distribution, Marketing, Business Affairs, and Entertainment Law)[1].

Regarding catalog policy updates, the **Miami campus closed its operations on May 15, 2023**, as stated in NYFA's official announcements and academic catalogs reflecting the consolidation to other locations like Los Angeles and New York[3][4].

## References
[1] https://www.educatly.com/program/552377/online-ma-in-entrepreneurial-producing-and-innovation  
[3] https://www.shiksha.com/studyabroad/usa/universities/new-york-film-academy  
[4] https://www.nyfa.edu/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Spring-2025_DOD_Rates.pdf
rounds.answer_b:
The New York Film Academy's relevant program is the **Online Master of Arts in Film and Media Production** [1]. This degree is structured for students pursuing entrepreneurial careers in film and television, with final requirements including a thesis film and a professional e-portfolio [1].

The production curriculum specifies several hands-on classwork subjects: **Hands-On Directing, Cinematography, Producing, Production Workshops, Screenwriting, Editing, Sound Design, and Production Design** [1].

For historical and theoretical support, the institution lists courses including **Aesthetics of Film, Film History, Documentary Film History, History of Television, and Narrative Film History** [1].

Regarding the Miami campus, NYFA's catalog policy updates officially closed the location on **August 23, 2019** [2]. This closure consolidated operations to other domestic and international campuses while maintaining online program offerings. The announcement specified that all current Miami students would be accommodated to complete their programs through alternative NYFA locations or online formats [2].

## References
[1] New York Film Academy. "Online Master of Arts in Film and Media Production: Curriculum." NYFA website. Accessed 2025.
[2] New York Film Academy. "Catalog Policy Updates: Miami Campus Closure." NYFA website. Accessed 2025.
